title: Engine operated by the archimedes force
abstract: The present invention relates to the engine operated by the Archimedes force, comprising: - a plurality of the floats (3) communicatively connected to each other to form a completely closed float chain wherein the total volume of air inside the chain is constant; - gaps defined between first float redirecting rails (5.1) and corresponding sections of belts or chains (4), the said gaps tend to enlarge gradually so that whenever the floats in the deflated state underneath the first driven wheels (9.1) are emerging, the said floats will be inflated by air from the floats above, whereby the pressure in the float chain is constantly maintained; - gaps defined between first float redirecting rails (5.2) and corresponding sections of the belts or chains (4), the said gaps tend to narrow gradually so as to deflate gradually the floats and expel the air inside the floats back to the next floats being emerging upward; and - driving wheels (15) with arms (14) which are pushed by force transmission pins (2.1) attached to each float when the floats (3) are immerging downward in the case of the driving wheels (15) coaxially attached to the driven wheels (9.1), or when the floats (3) are emerging upward in the case of the driving wheels (15) independently attached to the driven wheels (9.1), whereby a main shaft (6) of the engine is rotated.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS 1. A engine operated by the Archimedes force, comprising: a plurality of the floats (3) communicatively connected to each other to form a completely closed float chain wherein the total volume of air inside the chain is constant; first driven wheels (9.1) in combination with corresponding first float redirecting rails (5.1) for driving the floats being immerging in liquid, so as to redirect the said floats to the upward emerging side thereof, wherein the gaps defined between the first float redirecting rails (5.1) and the corresponding sections of belts or chains (4) located on surfaces of the first driven wheels (9.1) tend to enlarge gradually in the emerging direction of the floats so that whenever the floats in the deflated state underneath the first driven wheels (9.1) are emerging, the said floats will be inflated by air from the floats above, whereby the pressure in the float chain is constantly maintained; second driven wheels (9.2) in combination with corresponding second float redirecting rails (5.2) for pressing and driving the floats being emerging upward toward the top of the engine, so as to redirect the said floats to the downward immersing side thereof, wherein the gaps defined between the second float redirecting rails (5.2) and the corresponding sections of the belts or chains (4) located on surfaces of the second driven wheels (9.2) tend to narrow gradually in the emerging direction of the floats (3) so as to deflate gradually the floats and expel the air inside the floats back to the next floats being emerging upward; emerging float guiding rails (5) for guiding the floats (3)in the inflated state to emerge upward; immerging float guiding rails (8) for guiding the floats (3)in the deflated state to immerge into liquid; and driving wheels (15) with arms (14) which are pushed by force transmission pins (2.1) attached to each float when the floats (3) are immerging downward in the case of the driving wheels (15) coaxially attached to the first driven wheels (9.1), or when the floats (3) are emerging upward in the case of the driving wheels (15) independently 

 attached to the first driven wheels (9.1), whereby a main shaft (6) of the engine is rotated. 2. The engine according to claim 1 further comprising a float chain tension adjusting mechanism including positioning rods (9.22) attached to a shaft of the second driven wheels (9.2), wherein the said positioning rods can move up and down. 3. The engine according to claim 2, wherein the float chain tension adjusting mechanism further comprises rollers (1 1) to press the belts or chains (4) on the downward immerging float side. 4. The engine according to any one of claims 1 to 3 further comprising a mechanism for automatically adjusting the rotational speed of the engine that is an air containing expansible box (10) located among the second driven wheels (9.2), the first driven wheels (9.1) and the float chain, the air containing expansible box (10) comprises an one-way air inlet valve (12) and an one-way air outlet valve (13), wherein the air charging in or the air discharging out of the air containing expansible box (10) is controlled by an automatic electronic controller (DK). 5. The engine according to claim 4 further comprising a balance weight (20) with a density higher than that of water, wherein the balance weight (20) can be lowered down or lifted up in order to change the water levels in the engine depending on the requirement of increasing or decreasing the rotational speed of the engine. 6. The engine according to any one of the preceding claims further comprising a braking mechanism which comprises: an endless screw (16) which is rotated by a step engine (17); first cranks (18) each has one end attached to a first screw (18.1), which is engaged with the endless screw (16), and the other end attached to the emerging float guiding rail (5), respectively; and 

 second cranks (19) each has one end attached to a second screw (19.1), which is engaged with the endless screw (16), and the other end attached to the immerging float guiding rail (8), respectively; when received a stop signal from the automatic electronic controller (DK), the step engine (17) rotates the endless screw (16) which enables the first cranks (18) to press the emerging float guiding rails (5) moving nearer toward the first driven wheels (9.1) so that the floats being emerging cannot receive more the air from the other floats above, simultaneously the second cranks (19) push the immerging float guiding rails (8) to be far away from the second driven wheels (9.2) so that the floats being immerging can receive more the air expelled from the side of the pressed emerging floats, the engine will be stopped when the volumes on both sides are balanced. 7. The engine according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the driving wheels (15) are attached to the first driven wheels (9.1). 8. The engine according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the driving wheels (15) are independently and non-coaxial attached to the first driven wheels (9.1) and coupled only to both the driven wheels (9.1) and (9.2) through the belts or chains (4). 9. The engine according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein each float (3) comprises two layers, an inner layer (3.1) and an outer layer (3.2), wherein the inner layer (3.1) is made of rubber or plastic to contain air and the outer layer (3.2) is a hard shell that withstands the force and protects the inner layer, the outer layer comprises hard plates dynamically linked together or also to a float coupling (2, 3.6), thereby each float (3) can be deflated or inflated. 10. The engine according to claim 9, wherein the hard plates are dynamically linked together by hinges (3.3), or by elastic connecting plates (3.3'). 11. The engine according to claim 9 or 10, wherein the inner layers (3.1) of each float are integrally molded to form the float chain as a closed loop, wherein the floats are communicatively connected to each other directly at throat positions in the gaps of the float couplings (2) between the two adjacent floats. 12. The engine according to claim 9 or 10, wherein the inner layers (3.1) of each float of the float chain are made separately, and then airtight and communicatively connected through the gaps of the float couplings (2) between the two adjacent floats. 13. The engine according to claim 9 or 10, wherein the inner layers (3.1) of each float of the float chain are made separately, and then communicatively connected indirectly through connection tubes (3.5) located between the floats (3) and being outside the float chain. 14. The engine according to any one of claims 1 to 13 further comprising a float pressing roller (7) which is provided above the second driven wheels (9.2) to assist in discharging the air in the floats being pressed back to the next floats emerging upward. 15. The engine according to any one of claims 1 to 14 further comprising a engine casing (1) which bears and protects the components inside the engine. 16. The engine according to claim 15, wherein the engine casing (1) is a tank made of metal, composite or concrete to contain the liquid.
